2606.13174.title: Getting Better at Working With You: Compiling User Corrections into Runtime Enforcement for Coding Agents
2606.13174.paper_summary: Interactive LLM agents are becoming part of daily work, but they do not reliably become easier to work with over time: a correction remembered in one session may still be violated in the next. We study this gap between preference access and preference compliance. In tasks derived from anonymized real-user friction cases, Mem0 memory still leaves 57.5% of applicable preference checks violated. We introduce Test-time Rule Acquisition and Compiled Enforcement (TRACE), a drop-in skill-layer pipeline for coding-agent runtimes that mines user corrections, rewrites them as atomic rules, and compiles them into runtime checks that must pass before an agent completes future tasks. Unlike runtime checks written ahead of time by developers, TRACE skills come from the user's own chat corrections. We evaluate TRACE with simulated user-in-the-loop experiments on ClawArena coding-agent tasks and MemoryArena-derived memory-intensive tasks. On ClawArena, TRACE reduces held-out preference violation from 100.0% to 37.6% on in-distribution tasks and from 100.0% to 2.0% on out-of-distribution tasks. On MemoryArena-derived tasks, TRACE reduces in-distribution violation from 100.0% to 60.5% while matching or exceeding the strongest memory baseline on task pass. These results suggest that compiling corrections into runtime enforcement can address a repeated-friction failure mode that memory alone does not reliably solve, reducing the need for users to restate the same correction across future sessions. Experiment code is available at https://github.com/YujunZhou/TRACE_exp, and the deployable skill is available at https://github.com/YujunZhou/tellonce.
2606.13174.paper_summary_zh: 互動式 LLM 代理正在成為日常工作的組成部分，但它們並不會隨著時間的推移而變得更容易使用：在一個會話中記住的修正可能在下一次會話中仍然會被違反。我們研究了偏好訪問與偏好遵循之間的差距。在源自匿名真實用戶摩擦案例的任務中，Mem0 記憶仍然有 57.5% 的適用偏好檢查被違反。我們引入了測試時規則獲取和編譯執行 (TRACE)，這是一個用於編碼代理運行時的即插即用技能層管道，該管道挖掘用戶的修正，將其重寫為原子規則，並編譯成運行時檢查，這些檢查必須在代理完成未來任務之前通過。與開發者提前編寫的運行時檢查不同，TRACE 技能來自用戶自己的聊天修正。我們通過在 ClawArena 編碼代理任務和基於 MemoryArena 的記憶密集型任務上進行模擬用戶參與的實驗來評估 TRACE。在 ClawArena 上，TRACE 將保留的偏好違反從 100.0% 降低到 37.6%，在分佈內任務中，並將其從 100.0% 降低到 2.0% 在分佈外任務中。在基於 MemoryArena 的任務中，TRACE 將分佈內的違反從 100.0% 降低到 60.5%，同時在任務通過率上匹配或超過最強的記憶基準。這些結果表明，將修正編譯成運行時執行可以解決記憶單獨無法可靠解決的重複摩擦失敗模式，減少用戶在未來會話中重述相同修正的需求。實驗代碼可在 https://github.com/YujunZhou/TRACE_exp 獲得，並且可部署的技能可在 https://github.com/YujunZhou/tellonce 獲得。
2606.13174.author: Yujun Zhou et.al.
2606.13174.authors: Yujun Zhou, Kehan Guo, Haomin Zhuang, Xiangqi Wang, Yue Huang, Zhenwen Liang, Pin-Yu Chen, Tian Gao, Nuno Moniz, Nitesh V. Chawla, Xiangliang Zhang
